name: five_tasks_gather_pattern
description: main() coroutine must start all 5 tasks with asyncio.gather() in correct startup order
prompt: Write the main() coroutine that starts all 5 TradeOS tasks concurrently using asyncio.gather(). The tasks are: ws_listener, signal_processor, order_monitor, risk_watchdog, and heartbeat.

expectations.text: All 5 tasks are started: ws_listener, signal_processor, order_monitor, risk_watchdog, heartbeat
Tasks are created in correct startup order: risk_watchdog first, then order_monitor, ws_listener, signal_processor, heartbeat last
Uses asyncio.gather() or equivalent to run tasks concurrently
asyncio.gather() uses return_exceptions=True to prevent one crash killing all tasks
Includes reconciliation step that completes BEFORE tasks are started

name: blocking_io_uses_to_thread
description: order_monitor must use asyncio.to_thread() for kite.orders() and asyncio.sleep(5)
prompt: Write the order_monitor task that polls kite.orders() every 5 seconds and updates the order state machine.

expectations.text: Uses asyncio.to_thread(kite.orders) — not kite.orders() directly
Uses asyncio.sleep(5) — not time.sleep(5)
No synchronous API calls directly in the event loop (no kite.orders(), kite.positions() etc. without to_thread)
Handles API errors with try/except and continues polling — does NOT stop monitoring on error
Updates shared_state['open_orders'] with fetched order data

name: queue_overflow_drops_oldest
description: tick queue producer must drop oldest tick (not block) when queue is full
prompt: Write the tick queue producer for TradeOS that handles a full queue without blocking the ws_listener.

expectations.text: Checks queue.full() before attempting to put a new tick
If full: calls queue.get_nowait() to discard the oldest item first
Logs WARNING 'queue_overflow' when discarding (structlog, not print)
Never blocks the producer — does not use await queue.put() when full without first making room
Includes the queue depth in the overflow log (queue.maxsize or queue.qsize())

name: risk_watchdog_triggers_kill_switch
description: risk_watchdog must check daily loss every 1 second and trigger kill switch on breach
prompt: Write the risk_watchdog task body that checks daily loss every 1 second and triggers the kill switch when MAX_DAILY_LOSS_PCT is breached.

expectations.text: Uses asyncio.sleep(1) loop — checks every 1 second
Reads daily_pnl_pct from shared_state and compares to MAX_DAILY_LOSS_PCT (0.03)
Calls kill_switch.trigger(level=2) when daily_pnl_pct <= -MAX_DAILY_LOSS_PCT
Logs CRITICAL (not just ERROR) on its own crash — risk_watchdog crash is a critical event
Does NOT auto-restart after crash — crash triggers Level 3 kill switch, not a quiet restart

name: startup_sequence_order_enforced
description: Startup must ensure reconciliation completes and WS is connected before signal_processor starts
prompt: Write the startup sequence that ensures reconciliation completes before any trading task starts, and that ws_listener connects before signal_processor begins.

expectations.text: Reconciliation called with await before any asyncio.create_task() calls
ws_listener is started (create_task) BEFORE signal_processor
A gate blocks signal_processor until ws_connected=True (polling loop or asyncio.wait or similar)
risk_watchdog is started before ws_listener (first task created)
Startup mismatch aborts boot — system does not start trading if reconciliation finds mismatches

name: crash_recovery_wrapper
description: resilient_task wrapper must auto-restart all tasks except risk_watchdog which triggers Level 3
prompt: Write the resilient_task wrapper that auto-restarts crashed tasks, and a separate risk_watchdog variant that triggers Level 3 kill switch instead of restarting.

expectations.text: resilient_task uses while True loop with try/except for auto-restart
asyncio.CancelledError is re-raised — not caught and suppressed
Waits asyncio.sleep(5) before restarting after a crash
Updates shared_state['tasks_alive'][name] = False on crash, True after restart
risk_watchdog variant calls kill_switch.trigger(level=3) on crash — no restart

name: graceful_shutdown_sequence
description: SIGTERM handler must set Level 1, wait for orders (max 30s), reconcile, cancel tasks, send Telegram
prompt: Write the SIGTERM handler for graceful shutdown of the TradeOS trading system.

expectations.text: First action: sets kill switch Level 1 to stop new signals
Waits up to 30 seconds for open orders using asyncio.wait_for() with timeout=30
Runs final reconciliation before cancelling tasks
Cancels tasks in reverse startup order (heartbeat first, risk_watchdog last)
Sends Telegram daily summary as part of shutdown sequence

name: heartbeat_detects_dead_task
description: heartbeat task must detect dead tasks via tasks_alive dict and alert via Telegram
prompt: Write the heartbeat task that detects when another task has stopped running and emits system state every 30 seconds.

expectations.text: Checks shared_state['tasks_alive'] dict every 30 seconds for False entries
Logs CRITICAL when a task's alive status is False
Sends Telegram WARNING alert for dead task detection
Emits 'system_heartbeat' log with queue depths (tick_q, order_q qsize), ws_connected, kill_switch_level, daily_pnl_pct
Uses asyncio.sleep(30) — 30 second interval
